system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

);

(

(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phid,p)     Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(meshPhi,p)  Gauss upwind;
    div((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U)))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.reactingFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)

file: /global/cfs/cdirs/m4756/theseus/OpenFOAM-10/etc/codeTemplates/dynamicCode/fluidReactionThermo from line 17 to line 99.

    From function void Foam::compileTemplate::setFilterVariable(Foam::dynamicCode&, const Foam::dynamicCodeContext&, const Foam::Pair<Foam::word>&) const
    in file db/dynamicLibrary/compileTemplate/compileTemplate.C at line 81.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

This indicates that in **OpenFOAM 10** (as seen from the path `/global/cfs/cdirs/m4756/theseus/OpenFOAM-10/`), the `reactingMixture` mixture type is **no longer supported**. The Foundation version of OpenFOAM 10 restructured how thermophysical properties work — the old-style `reactingMixture` has been replaced.

## Root Cause

Looking at the current configuration files, especially `physicalProperties`, the following entries need to be updated:
- In `physicalProperties`: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to one of the supported mixture types.
- In `thermo.compressibleGas`: Same issue — the `mixture` entry needs updating.
- Additionally, the `specie` sub-dictionary may also need restructuring.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Update `constant/physicalProperties`

Change the `mixture` entry from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}
```

Also ensure the `specie` sub-dictionary contains all species listed in the `species` file (`CH4`, `O2`, `H2O`, `CO2`, `N2`), each mapping to their thermodynamic data. If the `specie` dictionary is missing or incomplete, add it with proper coefficients matching those referenced in `thermo.compressibleGas`.

### 2. Update `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`

Similarly, change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;` in the `thermoType` block within `thermo.compressibleGas`. Also verify the `specie` dictionary includes all five species with correct thermodynamic coefficients.

### 3. Verify `constant/reactions`

Ensure the `reactions` file references the same species names exactly as they appear in the `specie` dictionary in both `physicalProperties` and `thermo.compressibleGas`. The species list `(CH4 O2 H2O CO2 N2)` should match.

### Summary

The core fix is replacing `reactingMixture` with `multiComponentMixture` throughout the thermophysical property files (`physicalProperties` and `thermo.compressibleGas`). This is consistent with OpenFOAM 10's architectural changes where the Foundation version moved away from the legacy `reactingMixture` type to the more generic `multiComponentMixture` approach for handling reacting flows. All other configuration files (boundary conditions, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution, chemistryProperties, combustionProperties, momentumTransport) appear correctly configured and do not need modification.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'constant/physicalProperties', 'changes': 'Change mixture from reactingMixture to multiComponentMixture; Add specie sub-dictionary with entries for CH4, O2, H2O, CO2, N2 containing proper thermodynamic coefficients for janaf model; Ensure inertSpecie entry is N2'}, {'file': 'constant/thermo.compressibleGas', 'changes': 'Change mixture from reactingMixture to multiComponentMixture; Add complete specie sub-dictionary with CH4, O2, H2O, CO2, N2 entries containing valid janaf thermodynamic coefficients; Keep inertSpecie as N2'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.2 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
